---
title: "Adapter Validation Suites"
subtitle: "Per-adapter validation tests, evidence expectations, and drift-detection procedures"
doc-type: listing-master
canon-source: "uiao-core/canon/adapter-registry.yaml · uiao-core/canon/modernization-registry.yaml"
derived-from: "uiao-core canon (sync_canon.py)"
listing:
  id: adapter-validations
  type: table
  contents:
    - "*/avs-*.qmd"
  fields:
    - title
    - adapter-id
    - adapter-class
    - mission-class
    - status
    - phase
  sort:
    - "adapter-class asc"
    - "adapter-id asc"
  filter-ui: true
  sort-ui: true
  page-size: 50
  categories: true
---

# Adapter Validation Suites {.unnumbered}

This tree holds one **Adapter Validation Suite (AVS)** per adapter registered in `uiao-core` canon. Each AVS describes the validation tests, evidence expectations, and drift-detection procedures for its adapter. AVS documents are the runtime-verifiable counterpart to the design-time [Adapter Technical Specifications](../adapter-technical-specifications/index.qmd).

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived tree

Every AVS in this tree is scaffolded by `uiao-core/tools/sync_canon.py` from the two adapter registries. Frontmatter metadata is regenerated from canon on every sync and must not be hand-edited. Authored content lives under the `## Overview` heading and below in each document.
:::

## What an AVS contains

Each AVS document stubs the following sections, to be authored against the companion ATS:

| Section | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **Overview** | What this suite validates, scoped to the adapter's declared operational class and mission class. |
| **Scope** | Which surfaces / subsystems are in and out of scope for this suite. |
| **Controls** | Which NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls the suite produces evidence for. |
| **Operational profile** | Runtime, runner class, tenancy, evidence class, retention — inherited from canon. |
| **Canon invariants** | `gcc-boundary`, `ssot-mutation: never`, `certificate-anchored: true`, `object-identity-only: true`. |

## Relationship to the Validation Operations Playbook

AVS documents are the per-adapter instantiation of the cross-cutting validation discipline described in the (forthcoming) Validation Operations Playbook. Individual AVS entries should cite the playbook for shared procedures and only document adapter-specific deviations inline.

## All validation suites

The table below is generated automatically by Quarto from the AVS documents in this tree. Filter and sort controls are active.

:::{#adapter-validations}
:::

---

*Scaffolded by `uiao-core/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao-core/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract.*
